message:
fix(raptorq): preserve stream error semantics through typed mapping and add c=1 dual-policy benchmarks

poll_next_blocking previously flattened all SymbolStream errors into
ErrorKind::StreamEnded, losing the distinction between authentication
failures, protocol errors, timeouts, resets, cancellations, and I/O
errors. The new map_stream_error function translates each StreamError
variant to the most specific ErrorKind available:

- StreamError::AuthenticationFailed -> ErrorKind::CorruptedSymbol
- StreamError::ProtocolError -> ErrorKind::ProtocolError
- StreamError::Reset -> ErrorKind::ConnectionLost
- StreamError::Timeout -> ErrorKind::ThresholdTimeout
- StreamError::Cancelled -> ErrorKind::Cancelled
- StreamError::Io with TimedOut -> ErrorKind::ThresholdTimeout
- StreamError::Io with ConnectionRefused -> ErrorKind::ConnectionRefused
- StreamError::Io with InvalidData/InvalidInput -> ErrorKind::ProtocolError
- StreamError::Io (other) -> ErrorKind::ConnectionLost
- StreamError::Closed/PolledAfterCompletion -> ErrorKind::StreamEnded

This allows callers to make informed retry vs fail-closed decisions
based on the actual failure mode rather than treating everything as
a stream end.

Existing tests updated to use single-block params (num_source_blocks=1)
to match the corrected ObjectParams::min_symbols_for_decode threshold
from the prior commit.

Tests added for each StreamError variant: auth failure, protocol error,
reset, timeout, and cancellation mapping. ErrorStream test helper
introduced for constructing streams that yield a single error.

Benchmark additions: two new GF(256) dual-policy benchmark variants
for the c=1 (identity multiply) fast path — one using addmul_slices2
with Gf256::ONE, and a sequential baseline using gf256_add_slice for
comparison. The correctness validator also gained c=1 bit-exactness
checks to ensure the fast path produces identical results to the
sequential fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
